industry.headline: Cypress Creek Secures $3.5B for Steel River Energy Center
industry.summary: Cypress Creek Energy has secured $3.5 billion in financing for the first two phases of its Steel River Energy Center project. The US-based solar and energy storage developer will use the funds to advance construction of the large-scale renewable energy facility, marking a significant investment in clean energy infrastructure development.

industry.headline: MES Invites Bids for 6 MW Solar Projects O&M in Jodhpur
industry.summary: The Military Engineer Services has invited bids for operation and maintenance of 4 MW and 2 MW solar projects at the Army area in Jodhpur, Rajasthan. The tender seeks comprehensive O&M services for the military's solar installations to ensure optimal performance and reliability of the renewable energy systems.

industry.headline: Bihar Regulator Approves Tariff for 275 MW Rooftop Solar
industry.summary: The Bihar Electricity Regulatory Commission has approved tariffs for nearly 275 MW of rooftop solar power from approximately 250,000 Kutir Jyoti consumers across Bihar. The tariffs were discovered through competitive bidding process, enabling the state to procure renewable energy for rural households through its rooftop solar program.

industry.headline: Nagaland Bids for 1.65 MW Rooftop Solar Systems
industry.summary: The Department of Power in Nagaland has invited bids for 1.65 MW of grid-connected rooftop solar systems. These installations will be at village council halls under the Chief Minister's Community Solar Partnership Initiative. The Nagaland Solar Mission includes this program as part of its renewable energy strategy. Bids are due by June 24, 2026, with opening the following day.

industry.headline: NTPC Floats O&M Tender for 20 MW Gujarat Solar Project
industry.summary: NTPC has invited bids for two years of operation and maintenance of its 20 MW solar project. The facility is located at Jhanor Gandhar Gas Power Plant in Bharuch district, Gujarat. NTPC seeks qualified contractors to provide comprehensive O&M services for this solar installation. The tender supports ongoing operations of the state-owned energy giant's renewable assets.

industry.headline: KHS Machinery Meets 65% Power Needs with Solar Project
industry.summary: KHS Machinery has achieved 65% of its power requirements through rooftop solar. The Germany-based company installed a 750.2 kW solar project at its facility in Nandej, Gujarat. This installation will save the industrial equipment manufacturer ₹7.7 million annually. The rooftop solar system demonstrates how C&I consumers can reduce grid dependence and electricity costs.

tech.headline: Sierra Club Urges Climate Accountability in NYC Pension Asset Search
tech.summary: Sierra Club welcomes the search for asset managers for New York City’s five public pension systems announced by Comptroller Mark Levine and trustees. The organization urges that passive indexing service mandates be awarded only to firms demonstrating responsible stewardship and credible climate-risk management. The search opens a review of major asset managers to ensure strong climate accountability within the city's pension systems.

learn.title: Rooftop Solar & Net Metering in India
learn.intro: Rooftop solar allows households to generate clean energy, reducing bills and grid strain. Net metering credits excess power, accelerating India's renewable transition.
learn.c1_text: Solar panels convert sunlight into DC electricity, which inverters transform into AC for home use. Excess power flows to the grid, earning credits via net metering.
learn.c2_text: Under India's net metering policy, users receive credits for surplus energy exported to the grid, offsetting future bills. This incentivizes adoption, with over 10 GW installed nationwide.
learn.c3_text: India aims for 40 GW rooftop solar by 2026. States like Gujarat lead, with 3 GW installed, reducing carbon emissions by 8 million tons annually.
learn.stat2: Net metering saves households 20-30% on bills

learn.title: Small Wind Turbines for Homes
learn.intro: Small wind turbines harness local wind resources, providing decentralized power. Ideal for remote areas, they reduce reliance on fossil fuels.
learn.c1_text: Blades capture wind energy, spinning a generator to produce electricity. A 10 kW turbine generates ~10,000 kWh/year in 12 mph winds.
learn.c2_text: Turbines need consistent wind speeds (12+ mph) and clear zoning. Urban rooftops face turbulence, while rural sites yield higher output.
learn.c3_text: India has 7,000 MW small wind potential. Tamil Nadu pilot projects show 15% grid independence in remote villages.
learn.stat3: Tamil Nadu: 15% grid independence in villages
learn.stat4: Global: 10% turbine efficiency loss in turbulence

learn.title: Capacity Factor Explained
learn.intro: Capacity factor reveals how much energy a plant actually produces vs. its maximum potential, crucial for grid planning.
learn.c1_text: It's actual output divided by maximum potential. A 50% factor means a plant runs at half capacity yearly.
learn.c2_text: Wind/solar variability, maintenance, and weather reduce factors. Gas plants often exceed 85%, while wind averages 35%.
learn.c3_text: India's solar capacity factor averages 25%, lower than global 15-25% due to monsoon variability and dust.

learn.title: Smart Grids & Advanced Metering
learn.intro: Smart grids use digital tech to monitor and manage energy flow, integrating renewables and improving reliability.
learn.c1_text: Sensors and IoT devices enable real-time data. Two-way communication allows demand response, cutting peak loads by 15%.
learn.c2_text: Smart meters track usage hourly, enabling time-based pricing. California reduced peak demand by 5% with smart meters.
learn.c3_text: India aims for 200 million smart meters by 2025. Delhi's pilot cut outages by 40% using AI-driven fault detection.
learn.stat2: California: 5% peak reduction with smart meters

learn.title: The Duck Curve Challenge
learn.intro: High solar penetration causes evening demand spikes, straining grids. Solutions include storage and demand flexibility.
learn.c1_text: Solar's midday surplus creates a 'duck' shape in demand curves. California's 2020 grid faced 11 GW evening deficit.
learn.c2_text: Ramping gas plants up 10 GW/hour meets post-sunset demand. Battery storage can shave 30% of this need.
learn.c3_text: India plans 5 GW battery storage by 2025. Rajasthan uses pumped hydro to balance solar's duck curve.

learn.title: Second Life EV Batteries
learn.intro: Used EV batteries retain 70-80% capacity, offering cost-effective storage for grids and reducing waste.
learn.c1_text: After 8+ years in EVs, batteries store 70-80% of original capacity. A 60 kWh pack becomes 42 kWh for grid use.
learn.c2_text: Second-life storage costs 30-50% less than new. A 2023 study found $150/kWh vs. $450/kWh for fresh batteries.
learn.c3_text: IRENA projects 100 GWh second-life storage by 2030. Tata Motors repurposes 500+ EV batteries for microgrids.
